title: manihot carthagenensis var. anisophylla
intro: Manihot carthagenensis var. anisophylla, also known as bitter cassava or manioc, is a variety of the cassava plant, characterized by its bitter taste and smaller leaf size compared to other cassava varieties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Manihot carthagenensis var. anisophylla include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of juice, tincture, or tea, and is also used in traditional cooking as a flavoring agent and thickening agent.
